evidence: If n+1 objects are placed into n boxes, then at least one box contains at least two objects.
If N objects are distributed among k boxes, then some box contains at least ⌈N/k⌉ objects.
This principle is often used to prove existence results in discrete mathematics.

evidence: If \(n+1\) objects are placed into \(n\) boxes, then at least one box contains at least two objects.
If \(N\) objects are distributed among \(k\) boxes, then some box contains at least \(\left\lceil \frac{N}{k} \right\rceil\) objects.
